cartMetaDescription: Anonymous storefront cart backed by the live Darwin.WebApi public cart contracts.
checkoutMetaDescription: Public storefront checkout backed by live intent, order, and payment contracts.
confirmationMetaDescription: Storefront order confirmation and hosted checkout reconciliation view.
cartHeroTitle: Storefront cart is now a real public commerce slice
cartHeroDescription: This cart runs against the public Darwin.WebApi cart endpoints and keeps anonymous storefront identity stable through a dedicated web-owned cookie.
cartItemAdded: The item was added to the cart.
cartItemRemoved: The item was removed from the cart.
cartAddFailedMessage: The item could not be added to the cart.
cartUpdateInvalidMessage: The cart update request is invalid.
cartUpdateFailedMessage: The cart quantity could not be updated.
cartRemoveInvalidMessage: The cart remove request is invalid.
cartRemoveFailedMessage: The item could not be removed from the cart.
cartCouponMissingCartIdMessage: The coupon request is missing the cart identifier.
cartCouponApplyFailedMessage: The coupon could not be applied.
storefrontCartNotFoundMessage: The storefront cart resource was not found.
storefrontCartHttpErrorMessage: The storefront cart request could not be completed.
storefrontCartNetworkErrorMessage: The storefront cart API could not be reached.
cartDegradedTitle: Cart is running in degraded mode.
cartRouteSummaryMessage: Cart status: {status}. {itemCount} cart lines are currently visible. Follow-up product count: {followUpCount}.
emptyCartTitle: No items are in the storefront cart yet.
emptyCartDescription: Add a product from a detail page to create an anonymous storefront cart and validate the public cart contracts end to end.
couponPlaceholder: Enter coupon or leave blank to clear
checkoutSummaryNote: Public checkout now sits on top of this cart using the live intent, order-placement, and confirmation contracts from Darwin.WebApi.
followUpProductsDescription: The cart now keeps a small storefront follow-up rail visible so the shopper can keep browsing without losing the current cart context.
followUpProductFallbackDescription: Published storefront product available for quick follow-up from the cart.
followUpProductsUnavailableMessage: No additional published storefront products are available for follow-up right now.
cartNextStepsDescription: The storefront cart stays explicit about what the shopper should validate before leaving this page.
cartNextStepReviewMessage: Check quantities, applied coupon state, and line totals here before moving the shopper into authoritative checkout preview.
cartNextStepCheckoutTitle: Move to shipping and order preview
cartNextStepCheckoutMessage: Checkout uses the live intent contract to calculate shipping options and totals after address capture.
cartCrossSurfaceMessage: The cart should stay connected to the wider storefront system so the shopper can move between browsing, checkout, and account follow-up without losing context.
commerceContinuationMessage: Commerce routes should keep clear handoff paths into browsing, account follow-up, content context, and conversion recovery instead of ending in route-local CTA clusters.
commerceContinuationHomeDescription: Use the public home route to reorient around CMS, catalog, and member entry flows.
commerceContinuationCatalogDescription: Move back into published product discovery without losing the broader storefront context.
commerceContinuationCmsDescription: Use published content pages when the shopper needs broader storefront context beyond commerce routes.
commerceContinuationCartDescription: Go back to the current anonymous storefront cart to review quantities, coupon state, and line totals.
commerceContinuationAccountDescription: Keep self-service and member-portal follow-up visible alongside the storefront commerce flow.
checkoutUnavailableTitle: Checkout is unavailable without a cart.
checkoutUnavailableMessage: Add at least one published storefront product before starting the public checkout flow.
checkoutHeroTitle: Checkout now runs on the live storefront intent and order contracts
checkoutHeroDescription: Enter the delivery address, review the rated shipping options, and place an order through the public Darwin.WebApi checkout flow.
checkoutAddressIncompleteErrorMessage: Shipping and billing address fields are incomplete.
checkoutPlaceOrderFailedMessage: The order could not be placed.
checkoutHostedCheckoutStartFailedMessage: Hosted checkout could not be started.
checkoutPaymentCompletionFailedMessage: Hosted checkout return could not be reconciled.
storefrontCheckoutNotFoundMessage: The storefront checkout resource was not found.
storefrontCheckoutHttpErrorMessage: The storefront checkout request could not be completed.
storefrontCheckoutNetworkErrorMessage: The storefront checkout API could not be reached.
addressIncompleteMessage: Fill the required fields and refresh the checkout preview to calculate authoritative shipping and totals.
checkoutRouteSummaryMessage: Cart status: {cartStatus}. Intent status: {intentStatus}. {lineCount} cart lines are currently in scope. Address readiness: {addressReady}.
deliveryAddressDescription: This first slice keeps billing and shipping aligned to one address so the storefront checkout can stay small and contract-first.
shippingOptionsDescription: Select one option and refresh the preview before placing the order.
checkoutReadinessDescription: These signals reflect whether the current storefront draft is ready for authoritative preview and order placement.
shippingSelectionMissingMessage: Choose a shipping option in the preview form and refresh checkout before placing the order.
noPreviewMessage: The order button becomes available after the address is complete and the public checkout intent succeeds.
shippingNotRequiredMessage: The current checkout intent does not require a shipping selection for this order.
checkoutLinesDescription: The checkout summary now keeps the live cart lines visible so the shopper can verify products, quantities, and line totals before order placement.
placeOrderDescription: This creates the order through the public checkout endpoint and then moves the shopper into the confirmation and payment handoff flow.
checkoutCrossSurfaceMessage: Checkout is authoritative, but it should still stay connected to the rest of the storefront journey instead of becoming an isolated form route.
orderConfirmationUnavailableMessage: The public confirmation endpoint returned status "{status}".
orderConfirmationTitle: Order {orderNumber} is now on the storefront timeline
orderConfirmationDescription: This screen reads the authoritative storefront confirmation contract from Darwin.WebApi and keeps payment handoff visible instead of hiding it behind placeholder text.
orderPlacedMessage: The cart was successfully converted into an order. The next step is payment handoff unless the order is already marked as paid.
missingContextTitle: Payment return could not be finalized automatically.
missingContextMessage: The web-owned payment handoff context was missing, so the confirmation page can only show the latest order snapshot.
hostedCheckoutCancelledMessage: The shopper returned from the payment handoff without completing payment. The order remains visible here and payment can be retried.
confirmationRouteSummaryMessage: Confirmation status: {status}. Payment completion status: {paymentCompletionStatus}. Latest payment status: {paymentStatus}.
nextStepsDescription: The confirmation route now keeps the immediate follow-up actions visible so payment, account access, and order reference handling are explicit after checkout.
nextStepPaymentRetryMessage: Retry the hosted checkout handoff from this confirmation page to finish payment for the order.
nextStepPaymentPendingMessage: The order exists already, but payment is not recorded yet. Continue to the provider checkout to complete the storefront flow.
nextStepPaymentDoneMessage: No further payment step is required. The confirmation snapshot can now act as the stable handoff into order follow-up.
nextStepAccountSignedInTitle: Order follow-up is available in the member portal
nextStepAccountSignedInMessage: Because this browser already has a member session, you can move straight into the orders area for future tracking and payment follow-up.
nextStepAccountGuestTitle: Sign in if you want account-based tracking
nextStepAccountGuestMessage: The confirmation page remains public, but order history and invoice follow-up live in the member portal after sign-in or account creation.
nextStepReferenceMessage: Use the order number for support, payment retry, or future member-portal lookup so the storefront and support timelines stay aligned.
noPaymentAttemptsMessage: No payment attempts are attached yet.
paymentRecordedMessage: No additional payment handoff is required for this order.
confirmationCrossSurfaceMessage: The confirmation route should hand the shopper cleanly into browsing, account follow-up, or broader storefront orientation instead of ending as a receipt-only dead end.
